metadata.title:
Situations Wanted-Male

"CAP" INTRUDES UPON HIS
LITTLE SPEECH-HE DIS
APPEARS WITH BOR-


ROWED TOBACCO.

pages.raw_text:
Lewis LeClair, secretary of the
Union Card and Label league, has
a trophy of his fight for home in
dustry and for articles of commerce
bearing the union label, in the
shape of a can of "scab' tobacco.
He came by it in rather a singular
fashion, and is holding it at the
: Socks union hall for the man who
loaned ii to him this morning.


Here is how he got it and also
how he came to keep it.


: LeClair was walking along in the
vicinity of Main avenue and Stew
ems street this morning, when he
heard a man apparently with the
best of intentions, arguing in behalf
of home industry and the union
label. with a street corner crowd.
LeClair's ears bobbed up an inch
or two immediately-in short he
was interested. He listened in
tently while the man argued that
working men interested in their
own welfare should buy nothing
that did not contain the union la
bel. His little speech was eloquent
enough, but what was that in his
hand? AS LeClaire's piercing eye
rambled down the strangers form
